description: Edmonton Waste policies, 311 IVR scripts, CRM procedures, and collection rules.

questions.q: Residents must place their carts out by 7:00 AM on their scheduled collection day.
questions.info: Correct! Carts must be out by 7 AM to ensure they are not missed.

questions.q: The decrease in waste utility rates for 2026 is approximately 3%.
questions.info: Correct! Unlike property taxes, waste utility rates saw a slight decrease this cycle.

questions.q: Waste collection is cancelled on all statutory holidays.
questions.info: False! Collection occurs on all holidays except Christmas Day and New Year's Day.

questions.q: A 1-meter gap (3 feet) is required between carts and any obstacles like cars or fences.
questions.info: Correct! This 'rule of thumb' allows the automated truck arm to safely grab the cart.

questions.q: Diapers and pet waste should be placed in the Green Food Scraps cart.
questions.info: False! These items belong in the Black Garbage cart.

questions.q: Recyclables should be placed loosely in the Blue Bin/Cart, not inside tied plastic bags.
questions.info: Correct! Items must be loose so they can be sorted at the Materials Recovery Facility.

questions.q: Shredded paper should be placed in the Blue Recyclables cart.
questions.info: False! Shredded paper is too small for sorting machines; it should be placed in a clear plastic bag or composted.

questions.q: The City provides a 240L cart as the default size for both Garbage and Food Scraps.
questions.info: Correct! However, residents can request a smaller 120L garbage cart for a lower monthly fee.

questions.q: Residents can leave their carts on the curb or back alley all week long.
questions.info: False! Carts must be returned to the property by noon the day after collection.

questions.q: Standard residential waste collection includes unlimited 'extra' bags of garbage placed beside the cart.
questions.info: False! All garbage must fit inside the cart with the lid closed; extra bags require a 'tag' or a trip to an Eco Station.

questions.q: Aluminum foil and pie plates can be recycled if they are clean.
questions.info: Correct! As long as they are free of food residue, they are recyclable.

questions.q: Yard waste (leaves and branches) is collected on a seasonal schedule, not every week.
questions.info: Correct! Check the seasonal calendar for specific spring and fall cleanup dates.

questions.q: Plastic bags and stretchy film plastics are accepted in the Blue Recyclables cart.
questions.info: False! These materials are no longer accepted in the blue cart as of the current sorting rules.

questions.q: A resident who moves into a new house must pay for their first set of carts.
questions.info: False! Carts are assigned to the address, not the person. New builds receive their first set at no cost.

questions.q: Broken carts (wheels, lids) are repaired or replaced by the City at no additional charge.
questions.info: Correct! Maintenance is covered by the monthly utility fee unless the damage was intentional.

questions.q: The 'WasteWise' app can be used to set collection reminders and look up sorting rules.
questions.info: Correct! It is the primary tool we recommend to citizens for schedules.

questions.q: Hot ashes can be placed in the Black Garbage cart if they are at the bottom.
questions.info: False! Ashes must be fully extinguished and cooled for 48 hours to avoid cart fires.

questions.q: Small appliances like kettles or toasters can go in the Blue Recyclables cart.
questions.info: False! These are 'E-Waste' and must be taken to an Eco Station.

questions.q: Multi-family buildings (apartments/condos) always use the same green and black carts as houses.
questions.info: False! Many use large communal bins, though some are transitioning to the cart system.

questions.q: Glass jars and bottles are accepted in the Blue Recyclables cart.
questions.info: False! Glass should be taken to a Recycle Depot or Eco Station to prevent breakage and contamination.

questions.q: Styrofoam (polystyrene) is recyclable in the blue cart if it has the recycling symbol.
questions.info: False! Styrofoam is not accepted in Edmonton's blue cart program.

questions.q: Household Hazardous Waste (HHW) like paint and batteries can be dropped off for free at Eco Stations.
questions.info: Correct! There is no charge for residents to drop off HHW.

questions.q: The monthly waste fee is based on the amount of garbage produced, measured by weight.
questions.info: False! It is a flat monthly rate based on the size of the garbage cart selected.

questions.q: If a cart is missed, the resident should wait until next week to report it.
questions.info: False! Missed collections should be reported via 311 within 24 hours.

questions.q: Pizza boxes with heavy grease stains should be placed in the Green Food Scraps cart.
questions.info: Correct! Soiled paper and cardboard are compostable.

questions.q: Large furniture items can be left at the curb for regular weekly pickup.
questions.info: False! Large items require a scheduled Big Bin Event or a trip to an Eco Station.

questions.q: The 'Lid Closed' rule is to prevent litter and deter pests like magpies and coyotes.
questions.info: Correct! Overfilled carts with open lids will often be tagged and not collected.

questions.q: Construction waste (drywall, wood) can be placed in the Black Garbage cart.
questions.info: False! This must be taken to an Eco Station or a designated waste management facility.

questions.q: Residents can swap their 240L garbage cart for a 120L cart to save money.
questions.info: Correct! There is a one-time swap fee, but the monthly utility rate will decrease.

questions.q: Paper coffee cups are recyclable in the Blue Cart.
questions.info: False! Most have a plastic lining; they currently belong in the Black Garbage cart.

questions.q: Biodegradable or 'compostable' plastic bags are allowed in the Green Food Scraps cart.
questions.info: False! Edmonton's processing facility requires food scraps to be loose or in BPI-certified paper bags only.

questions.q: Grass clippings should be placed in the Green Food Scraps cart.
questions.info: False! Grass should be 'grasscycled' (left on the lawn) or put in clear bags for seasonal yard waste pickup.

questions.q: Residents can appeal a 'Missed Collection' if their cart was not out by 7:00 AM.
questions.info: False! Drivers log 'Not Out' carts, and 311 will not send a truck back if it was the resident's error.

questions.q: Cooking oil should be poured down the drain rather than put in a cart.
questions.info: False! Cooking oil should be put in a sealed container and taken to an Eco Station.

questions.q: Textiles (clothes, shoes) are accepted in the Blue Recyclables cart.
questions.info: False! These should be donated or taken to an Eco Station for textile recycling.

questions.q: The City of Edmonton uses an 'Assisted Waste Collection' program for residents with mobility issues.
questions.info: Correct! Residents can apply to have collectors pick up waste from their doorstep.

questions.q: Christmas trees are collected from the curb during a specific window in January.
questions.info: Correct! Real trees are collected for composting; all decorations must be removed.

questions.q: The 'See it, Report it' tool in the app is used for reporting illegal dumping.
questions.info: Correct! This helps the City track and clean up waste in public spaces.

questions.q: You can fit up to two 120L garbage carts at a single residential property.
questions.info: False! Only one garbage cart is permitted per household unit.

questions.q: Bubble wrap is recyclable in the blue cart.
questions.info: False! It is a film plastic and belongs in the garbage.

questions.q: The Edmonton Waste Management Centre is one of the most advanced in North America.
questions.info: Correct! It handles sorting, composting, and landfill operations.

questions.q: A 'Missed Collection' report must be filed within 2 business days of the incident.
questions.info: Correct! Reporting promptly ensures the service provider can return if necessary.

questions.q: Pet poop must be double-bagged before going into the Black Garbage cart.
questions.info: Correct! This is a safety requirement for waste collectors.

questions.q: Milk cartons and juice boxes should be rinsed before being recycled.
questions.info: Correct! Rinsing prevents contamination and odors.

questions.q: Metal coat hangers are allowed in the Blue Recyclables cart.
questions.info: False! These 'tanglers' damage the sorting equipment; they should go to an Eco Station.

questions.q: Residents in new neighborhoods might have 'Communal Collection' during construction.
questions.info: Correct! Temporary bins are often used until roads are safe for automated trucks.

questions.q: A tenant is always the one responsible for the waste utility bill.
questions.info: False! The waste utility is usually tied to the EPCOR water account, which may be in the landlord's name.

questions.q: Light bulbs (CFLs and LEDs) can be put in the garbage cart.
questions.info: False! These contain materials that must be handled at an Eco Station.

questions.q: The monthly waste fee includes access to the Big Bin Events held throughout the city.
questions.info: Correct! These events allow for free disposal of large items for residents.

questions.q: Soft plastics like bread bags go in the green cart.
questions.info: False! No plastics of any kind are allowed in the Green Food Scraps cart.

questions.q: In the 311 IVR menu, citizens should press '4' for Waste related inquiries.
questions.info: Correct! Option 4 is dedicated to waste, recycling, and new cart inquiries.

questions.q: The Waste Management Voice Digital Assistant is currently in a 'training' phase and learns from experience.
questions.info: Correct! The IVR script notes the digital assistant is in training to improve its interactions.

questions.q: Agents must create a CRM ticket for every waste-related call, even for general info.
questions.info: Correct! 311 agent instructions require creating a CRM ticket to track the interaction.

questions.q: The short code 'TB' is used for Edmonton Waste Management Centre route information.
questions.info: Correct! Route 589 inquiries are logged with the 'TB' short code.

questions.q: The Route 589 is a public route open to all citizens visiting the Waste Management Centre.
questions.info: False! It is a contracted service specifically for employees of the centre.

questions.q: Route 589 operates to and from the Coliseum Transit Centre.
questions.info: Correct! This is the primary transit link for the Waste Management Centre.

questions.q: If a citizen insists on leaving feedback about waste services, the agent must use CRM code X999.
questions.info: Correct! Feedback requiring management review uses X999 to trigger an email alert.

questions.q: 311 operating hours for the Waste queue are 7 AM to 7 PM, 7 days a week.
questions.info: Correct! This is the standard operating window for the contact centre.

questions.q: The 311 contact centre is open on all statutory holidays to assist with waste issues.
questions.info: False! The scripts state the centre is closed on statutory holidays.

questions.q: If a caller dialling 311 does not hear IVR options, it may be because they used a direct-dial number.
questions.info: Correct! Direct-dial numbers bypass the main IVR menu options.

questions.q: Waste collection scripts are originally authored in Microsoft Word before being converted to Google Docs.
questions.info: Correct! The documentation notes they are copies of original Word formats.

questions.q: Agents can plan a trip to the Waste Management Centre using the ETS Trip Planner.
questions.info: Correct! The centre is listed as a specific destination in the 'Other Routes' section of the script.

questions.q: The IVR option for 'Drainage' is the same as the option for 'Waste'.
questions.info: False! Waste is Option 4, while Drainage is Option 5.

questions.q: Personal information collected during a waste inquiry may be shared with vendors to fulfill service requests.
questions.info: Correct! The FOIP message states info may be shared with vendors for service efficiency.

questions.q: If a caller is abusive while reporting a missed collection, the agent must continue the call.
questions.info: False! Abusive language and harassment are not tolerated at the 311 centre.

questions.q: A 'Missed Collection' ticket requires the caller's phone number and name in the CRM Caller Tab.
questions.info: Correct! Standard instructions for all scenarios require these details.

questions.q: Agents should refer citizens to EPCOR for waste-related power outage inquiries.
questions.info: Correct! Power outages are handled by EPCOR, though the prompt is under the Drainage menu.

questions.q: The Waste Management Centre is serviced by 'On Demand Transit'.
questions.info: False! It is served by the contracted Route 589.

questions.q: The 311 IVR allows a caller to press '*' to repeat menu options.
questions.info: Correct! This is a standard navigation feature in the welcome message.

questions.q: General waste information provided without further action is logged as 'X998'.
questions.info: Correct! X998 is the short code for General Information ONLY.

questions.q: Agents can suggest that citizens use the 311 App to report waste issues after hours.
questions.info: Correct! The after-hours message directs citizens to the app or website.

questions.q: There is a specific hold message for Waste inquiries that plays while citizens wait.
questions.info: False! Hold messages (like Permits/Inspections) play across all skills.

questions.q: If a caller selects Option 4 but stays on the line without choosing a sub-option, they go to the Waste queue.
questions.info: Correct! Staying on the line after selecting the main category routes to an agent.

questions.q: The 'WasteWise' digital assistant can be accessed directly from the main 311 menu.
questions.info: False! It is Option 1 *after* pressing 4 for the Waste menu.

questions.q: Agents are authorized to change the hold music if a citizen complains about it.
questions.info: False! Hold music is part of the Cisco system and cannot be changed.

questions.q: The short code 'TP' is used if an agent plans a transit trip for a citizen to a recycling depot.
questions.info: Correct! 'TP' is the code for Schedule Information or Agent Planned Trips.

questions.q: A citizen can report a 'Public Safety' waste issue (like needles) to the after-hours line.
questions.info: Correct! After-hours, public safety matters are directed to 780-508-9200.

questions.q: All 311 calls are recorded for quality and training purposes.
questions.info: Correct! This is stated in both the business hours and FOIP automated messages.

questions.q: If a citizen asks why the City uses an IVR for waste, agents should mention 'improved service delivery'.
questions.info: Correct! The FAQ script lists improved delivery and faster response as key benefits.

questions.q: Agents should use 'Suggesting' mode when making any updates to the master scripts.
questions.info: Correct! This is a mandatory instruction for document maintenance.

questions.q: The Route 589 service for the Waste Management Centre is a 'Contracted Service'.
questions.info: Correct! The script explicitly identifies it as such.

questions.q: The short code 'T059' is used for providing the location of an Eco Station.
questions.info: Correct! T059 is for 'All Other General Information Provided'.

questions.q: 311 agents are expected to resolve inquiries politely and patiently.
questions.info: Correct! This standard is highlighted in the IVR message to callers.

questions.q: The Waste digital assistant system will 'learn' from its experience with the caller.
questions.info: Correct! This is part of the automated greeting for the assistant.

questions.q: If a caller wants to know about personal info collection, they should press Option 7.
questions.info: Correct! Option 7 explains the collection and use of personal information.

questions.q: A 'CRM Short Code' must be chosen for every single call scenario.
questions.info: Correct! This is a standard step in agent instructions for all scripts.

questions.q: The 311 script version dated 'November 20, 2024' is the most recent for IVR info.
questions.info: Correct! This is the 'as of' date listed in the IVR documentation.

questions.q: Agents should email '311Scripting@edmonton.ca' to request an updated copy of a script.
questions.info: Correct! This is the standard procedure for script changes or copies.

questions.q: The IVR message for Waste inquiries is only available in English.
questions.info: Correct! Current scripts do not mention automated translation for the IVR menu.

questions.q: A caller can return to the previous menu by pressing the '#' key.
questions.info: False! The script specifies the '*' key to go back or repeat.

questions.q: The FOIP message is played *after* an agent answers the call.
questions.info: False! It is an automated message played prior to being transferred to an employee.

questions.q: Waste-related inquiries include 'new Cart inquiries'.
questions.info: Correct! This is explicitly listed as a reason to select Option 4.

questions.q: Agents must summarize caller feedback in the 'Problem Description' field in CRM.
questions.info: Correct! This is required when citizens insist on leaving feedback.

questions.q: An 'Urgent Internal' number exists for priority access, but it is not for public use.
questions.info: Correct! The FAQ confirms it is for internal department use ONLY.

questions.q: The 'MoveLearnPlay' website is mentioned in the Waste IVR menu.
questions.info: False! That website is for Attractions and Recreation (Option 2).

questions.q: Agents are instructed *not* to create a copy of the Google version of the scripts.
questions.info: Correct! This is a bolded warning at the top of the script documents.

questions.q: The IVR Flow Chart link in the script is an internal link and should not be shared.
questions.info: Correct! Many internal links are marked 'do NOT share'.

questions.q: If a citizen is concerned their info was shared with EPCOR, agents should refer to the FOIP statement.
questions.info: Correct! Sharing with 'public bodies' like EPCOR is covered under the FOIP authority.

questions.q: The short code 'T055' is used for providing a Waste Collection map.
questions.info: Correct! T055 is used for System Map Information.

questions.q: Agents should provide their CRM ticket number to a support team member during escalation.
questions.info: Correct! This is a required step for proper escalation tracking.
